notes: Set thine house in order - i.e. make arrangement as to the succession to the throne; for he had then no son

quotes.text: Hezekiah's illness made him ritually unclean, preventing him from entering the temple, so he turned his face toward the temple wall to pray. Before his illness, Hezekiah prayed in the temple (37:14-15).

notes: How often our wishes, when gratified, prove curses! Hezekiah lived to have a son; that son was the idolater Manasseh, the chief cause of God's wrath against Judah, and of the overthrow of the kingdom (2Ki 23:26, 27).
wept sore [JOSEPHUS says, the reason why he wept so sorely was that being childless, he was leaving the kingdom without a successor

JOSEPHUS thinks these degrees were steps ascending to the palace of Ahaz; the time of day was indicated by the number of steps reached by the shadow because HERODOTUS (2.109) states that the sundial and the division of the day into twelve hours, were invented by the Babylonians.
cutting off of my days - "the meridian"; when the sun stands in the zenith
cutting off of my days - "the meridian"; when the sun stands in the zenith
a lion, so will he break all my bones ["I made myself like a lion (namely, in roaring, through pain
Clarifies the meaning of the first clause: "Behold, I had great bitterness instead of peace."
pit of corruption, for thou hast cast all my sins behind thy back.
After an illness that had kept one from entering the temple was cured, it was customary to wash and go before the priests to be pronounced clean.
